name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax provides short-term vehicle title loans and title pawns, letting borrowers use their car or motorcycle title as collateral while keeping the vehicle.
description_long:
TitleMax was founded when its first store opened September 1, 1998, in Columbus, Georgia. Originally part of TMX Finance, the company was subsequently acquired by Community Choice Financial (CCF Holdings). Headquartered in Savannah, Georgia, with additional corporate offices in Dallas, Texas, TitleMax has grown to approximately 900 locations across roughly 13 states. The company holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications and is not BBB accredited. In Texas it operates under the state's Credit Access Business (CAB) regulatory framework, as mandated by state law.

TitleMax's primary product is the vehicle title loan — a short-term secured loan in which the borrower pledges their car or motorcycle title as collateral while retaining use of the vehicle. Loan amounts range from approximately $601 to $10,000, depending on vehicle value, state regulations, and borrower income. In Georgia, TitleMax also offers title pawns, which are legally distinct from loans but functionally similar. In select states such as South Carolina, unsecured personal loans up to $2,500 are available. Applications can be completed in-store, online, or by phone, with funds disbursed in as little as 30 minutes. Neither a good credit score nor a bank account is required to apply.

TitleMax's central differentiator is accessibility: it explicitly targets consumers who cannot qualify for conventional bank products and does not require a bank account. With roughly 900 stores across 13 states, it operates one of the largest physical footprints in the title lending industry. A mobile app (iOS and Android) and an online customer portal at account.titlemax.com allow borrowers to view balances, due dates, and payment history and to make payments remotely. Speed is a core value proposition — the company advertises funding in as little as 30 minutes from application.

For consumers in acute financial distress with no other credit options, TitleMax can provide fast liquidity. However, the cost is severe. Third-party sources indicate APRs typically range from 100% to over 300%, with monthly fees that can reach approximately 25% of the borrowed amount — turning a short-term loan into a potentially long and expensive obligation. TitleMax's regulatory history includes fines for misrepresenting loan terms, overcharging interest, and administrative fee violations. Consumer complaint volumes on BBB and ConsumerAffairs are substantial, with recurring themes of repossession disputes and data-breach concerns. Failure to repay risks losing the vehicle outright, a devastating consequence for borrowers who depend on their car. This product is best approached as a last resort and only after fully understanding the total cost of borrowing.
address: 4941 Walzem Rd Suite A, Windcrest, TX 78218

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. Title loans are short-term secured loans; terms are set at origination.

pros: Funds available in as little as 30 minutes after approval
No credit score requirement — accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit history
No bank account required to obtain a loan
Borrower keeps and uses their vehicle throughout the entire loan term
Approximately 900 store locations across 13 states provide broad in-person access
Mobile app (iOS and Android) and online portal at account.titlemax.com for payments and account management
Founded in 1998 — over 25 years of continuous operation in the title lending industry
cons: APRs typically range from 100% to over 300%, making these among the most expensive consumer loan products available
Monthly fees can reach approximately 25% of the borrowed amount, compounding total repayment cost rapidly
Vehicle repossession is a real and documented risk if the borrower cannot repay on schedule
Regulatory history includes fines for misrepresenting loan terms, overcharging interest, and administrative fee violations
Only available in approximately 13 states — inaccessible to most of the U.S. population
Not BBB accredited; high volume of consumer complaints on BBB and ConsumerAffairs regarding repossession disputes and a data breach
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit history who need cash within hours
Unbanked or underbanked consumers who cannot access traditional financial institutions
Borrowers needing $601–$10,000 secured against a paid-off or nearly paid-off vehicle
Consumers facing a financial emergency after exhausting all lower-cost credit alternatives
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for vehicle owners with poor or no credit who face a genuine financial emergency and have no qualifying lower-cost alternative. The critical caveat is price: APRs of 100%–300%+ and monthly fees up to 25% of the loan amount make these loans extremely expensive, and defaulting puts the borrower at direct risk of permanently losing their vehicle.
services: Car title loans (collateral: vehicle title; borrower retains and uses vehicle)
Title pawns (Georgia locations only — legally distinct from loans)
Unsecured personal loans up to $2,500 (select states, including South Carolina)
Same-day funding in as little as 30 minutes
Online account portal (account.titlemax.com) — balance tracking, due dates, payment history
Mobile app (iOS and Android) for account management and payments
